other_observers: Dwayne Nelson from Las Animas; Mark Miller- Longmont
specific_location: Left Hand Creek off of Pike Rd. East. Creek runs along Left Hand Creek Park.
description: pale olive back, streaking on the chest and flanks, streaking was lacking on the throat area. very white supercillium and long reaching towardsthe back of the head( nape area), longer bill than Northern Waterthrush. Bubblegum pink legs. Habitat that it was found in suggests Louisiana over Northern, but you can't always speculate- especially during migration. But habitat does have an influence on where you find bird species.Behaviors: Bird was feeding along the waters edge picking at the surface. The bird was bobbing its tail up and down and to the side very slowly and fluidly. Much slower that the Northern Waterthrush. This bird was around its favorite habitat- Brushy banks with a running stream and low dwelling bushes.Call: no song that I heard, just a sharp "Chink" call as it was flushedPlumage: Breeding
